debt_items.description: BuildDetail.razor calls LogParser.ParseLogMessages(_job.Logs) on line 172 but ILogParser is never injected via @inject directive. This will cause NullReferenceException at runtime when attempting to view builds with logs.
debt_items.recommendation: Add '@inject ILogParser LogParser' to page directives before @code block
debt_items.title: Missing ILogParser Injection

debt_items.description: The DownloadLogs method generates a JavaScript string but never executes it. The method ends without code to invoke the JavaScript download functionality.
debt_items.recommendation: Add code to evaluate and execute the generated JavaScript using IJavaScriptRuntime or proper DOM manipulation
debt_items.title: DownloadLogs Incomplete

debt_items.description: The worktree source is missing the required CSS classes: .build-log-container, .build-log-timestamp, .build-log-content, .build-log-empty, .build-log-cached, .build-log-actions. These must be defined in site.css to properly style the log widget.
debt_items.recommendation: Add CSS rules for all required log widget classes with max-height for scrolling, timestamp styling, and proper container styling
debt_items.title: Missing CSS Classes

debt_items.description: CopyLogs method copies data to clipboard but provides no visual feedback to the user via StateHasChanged() or toast notification.
debt_items.recommendation: Add toast notification or temporary button text change to confirm copy was successful
debt_items.title: CopyLogs Does Not Report Success

summary: The code implementation has a BLOCKING defect: ILogParser.ParseLogMessages() is called but ILogParser is never injected, causing a NullReferenceException at runtime. BuildDetail.razor references LogParser.ParseLogMessages(_job.Logs) but no @inject ILogParser LogParser exists in the page directives. Also, the DownloadLogs method generates JavaScript but doesn't execute it. Missing CSS classes (.build-log-container, .build-log-timestamp, .build-log-content, .build-log-empty, .build-log-cached, .build-log-actions) are not defined in site.css, causing unstyled elements.
